vibe: Saturday selections. UK jazz, Tokyo math-rock, Melbourne funk, Berlin techno. 10 tracks, no filler.

tracks.name: Woman's Touch (feat. Jamilah Barry)
tracks.reason: London jazz meets broken beat — Yussef Dayes is redefining UK jazz. Drummer-composer who makes you feel like you're in a Peckham basement at 2am.

tracks.name: Vocoder [Club Mix]
tracks.reason: Sam Shepherd bridges electronic and classical like nobody else. This one shimmers — minimal, precise, emotionally devastating.

tracks.name: (It Goes Like) Nanana
tracks.reason: Korean-born, Berlin-based DJ who crossed over massive. This track was everywhere in 2024 — instant party energy.

tracks.name: Red Room
tracks.reason: Melbourne future-soul. Nai Palm's voice is otherworldly — jazz, funk, electronic all twisted together. Anderson .Paak is a fan.

tracks.name: Daylight Savings
tracks.reason: Melbourne instrumental funk straight out of the 70s. All analog, no computers. Like finding a lost Meters record.

tracks.name: China
tracks.reason: Japanese punk-pop quartet who don't care about your genre rules. Kawaii meets noise-punk. They opened for Gorillaz.

tracks.name: So U Kno
tracks.reason: Welsh brothers making UK rave music that actually crosses over. This one builds and builds — proper euphoric.

tracks.name: Softly
tracks.reason: South London poet turned indie darling. Gentle, devastating lyrics over dreamy production. Gen-Z Sade energy.

tracks.name: Breathe
tracks.reason: London-raised, Afro-Cuban influenced jazz pianist. Hip-hop sensibility, jazz chops. Stones Throw vibes.

tracks.name: グッドバイ -album version-
tracks.reason: Japanese math-rock legends. Intricate guitar work that somehow feels emotional, not technical. Essential J-rock.
